utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final plan: it includes health_coverage (with physical and mental health insurance and costs), property_coverage (home/contents with cost), travel_coverage (travel insurance with cost), additional_services (provided but not with explicit costs; it says none explicitly chosen and mentions inclusions without costs), and total_budget (monthly and annual costs). Next, verify if each item is fully specified per the format. Health_coverage: explicitly includes physical and mental health insurance with costs—fully specified. Property_coverage: home/contents insurance with cost—fully specified. Travel_coverage: travel insurance with cost—fully specified. Additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs; provided text indicates none explicitly chosen and mentions included services but no costs—this is not fully specified. Total_budget: includes monthly and annual breakdown—fully specified. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three specific items: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health insurance, LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health insurance, LifeFirst Standard Coverage for home contents insurance, and LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel insurance. I matched each to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings under primary_health_coverage (physical_health and mental_health), property_protection (home_content), and mobility_coverage (travel_insurance).
